name: Ghandruk
short_description: Gurung settlement and Annapurna trekking node.
full_description: Ghandruk is a picturesque traditional Gurung village situated at 2,012m. It serves as a key interface between lower agricultural zones and the Annapurna Base Camp trekking circuits, featuring stone architecture and the Gurung Cultural Museum.

name: Dhampus
short_description: Accessible ridge village with major mountain views.
full_description: Dhampus occupies a long ridge offering accessible views of Annapurna South and Machhapuchhre. It is a rest stop for the Mardi Himal trek and is located near the Australian Base Camp.

name: Sikles
short_description: Large Gurung settlement and eco-tourism gateway.
full_description: One of the largest Gurung settlements in Nepal, Sikles is a model for eco-tourism and community-led conservation. It acts as a gateway to the eastern Annapurna and Lamjung Himal regions.

name: Lwang
short_description: Eco-village known for tea gardens and canyoning.
full_description: Lwang is renowned for its organic, community-managed tea gardens and adventure tourism, including canyoning at the Kudi River waterfalls.

name: Astam
short_description: Hilltop eco-village focused on homestay tourism.
full_description: Meaning 'Sunset,' Astam is a serene hilltop village focused on sustainable eco-living, organic local products, and slow-paced farm interaction.

name: Begnas Lake
short_description: Freshwater lake with boating and resort tourism.
full_description: The second largest lake in the Pokhara Valley, Begnas is managed for irrigation and caged fisheries. It offers a tranquil alternative to Phewa Lake with mid-range resort facilities.

name: Rupa Lake
short_description: Protected freshwater lake with low-impact tourism.
full_description: A protected preservation area that remains free of commercial shoreline development, Rupa Lake supports over 36 species of waterbirds and an eco-friendly hiking circuit.

name: Jomsom
short_description: Trans-Himalayan hub in the Kali Gandaki corridor.
full_description: The administrative center of Mustang District, Jomsom is situated at 2,743m in the world's deepest gorge. It is a major hub for flights and the gateway to Upper Mustang.

name: Marpha
short_description: Preserved Mustang village known for apples and monastery culture.
full_description: Famous as the 'apple capital' of Nepal, Marpha features stone-lined streets, an efficient underground drainage system, and local distilleries producing apple brandy.

name: Kagbeni
short_description: Fortified confluence village and gateway to Upper Mustang.
full_description: A fortified village at the confluence of the Kali Gandaki and Jhong rivers, Kagbeni is home to the ancient Red Monastery and serves as the final checkpoint for Upper Mustang.

name: Muktinath
short_description: High-altitude sacred pilgrimage complex.
full_description: A sacred site for Hindus and Buddhists at 3,762m, Muktinath features 108 water spouts and an eternal flame burning on water.

name: Manang Village
short_description: High-altitude acclimatization hub on the Annapurna Circuit.
full_description: Situated at 3,519m, Manang is a principal stop on the Annapurna Circuit for acclimatization, offering access to Gangapurna Lake and Milarepa Cave.

name: Pisang
short_description: Traditional Annapurna trail settlement below Pisang Peak.
full_description: Divided into Upper and Lower settlements, Pisang offers superior views of Annapurna II and serves as a major trailhead for Pisang Peak.

name: Braga
short_description: Cliffside settlement with major monastery heritage.
full_description: A cliffside village featuring 'stacked' architecture, Braga is home to one of the oldest and most culturally significant monasteries in the region.

name: Ghalegaun
short_description: Model village known for Gurung homestay tourism.
full_description: Known as 'Asia's model tourism village,' Ghalegaun features the SAARC Village Tourism Museum and structured community homestays.

name: Bhujung
short_description: Dense Gurung hillside settlement with traditional architecture.
full_description: Bhujung is arguably the largest and most concentrated Gurung village, famous for its traditional honey-hunting culture and traditional stone housing.

name: Rainaskot
short_description: Historic fortified hill village with homestays and panoramic views.
full_description: The ancestral home of the Shah dynasty, Rainaskot provides views of 18 mountain ranges and has been rebuilt into high-quality community homestays.

name: Bandipur
short_description: Preserved Newar hill town and heritage tourism center.
full_description: A 'time-capsule' hill station featuring preserved 18th-century Newar architecture, neoclassical facades, and slate-paved streets.

name: Tansen
short_description: Historic administrative hill town of Palpa.
full_description: The administrative center of Palpa District, Tansen is famous for its Dhaka weaving industry, Tansen Durbar, and moderate year-round climate.

name: Sirubari
short_description: Pioneer community homestay village in Syangja.
full_description: Declared Nepal's first model tourist village in 2000, Sirubari is the birthplace of the community homestay movement, emphasizing equal income distribution.

name: Lo Manthang
short_description: The capital of the former Kingdom of Lo, a walled medieval city.
full_description: A remote settlement featuring a four-story royal palace and three major monasteries (Gompas). It is famous for its Tiji Festival and proximity to ancient man-made sky caves.

name: Tilicho Lake
short_description: One of the highest lakes in the world at 4,919 meters.
full_description: A glacial lake situated in the Annapurna range. Reaching the lake requires a side-trek from the Annapurna Circuit, passing through steep terrain and the Tilicho Base Camp.

name: Barpak
short_description: A large Ghale and Gurung village, known as the epicenter of the 2015 earthquake.
full_description: Barpak is a densely clustered village with stone-roofed houses perched on a steep hillside. It offers views of Buddha Himal and Sringi Himal and is known for its resilient community culture.

name: Laprak
short_description: A traditional Gurung village located near Barpak.
full_description: Located at an altitude of 2,100 meters, Laprak is a sister village to Barpak. It is known for its unique culture, traditional farming, and the integrated settlement built after the 2015 earthquake.

name: Mardi Himal High Camp
short_description: Final accommodation point before the Mardi Himal Base Camp.
full_description: Sitting at 3,580 meters, High Camp offers spectacular close-up views of Machhapuchhre (Fishtail) and the Annapurna range. It is the staging point for the hike to the Upper Viewpoint and Base Camp.

name: Dhorpatan Hunting Reserve
short_description: The only hunting reserve in Nepal.
full_description: Spanning over Baglung, Myagdi, and Rukum, the reserve is home to Blue Sheep (Naur) and is the only place in Nepal where licensed hunting is allowed. It also features beautiful meadows and high-altitude marshes.

name: Panchase Hill
short_description: A sacred hill and viewpoint shared by Kaski, Syangja, and Parbat.
full_description: Panchase is a biological diversity hotspot and a religious site. The peak (2,500m) offers views of five major peaks (Dhaulagiri, Annapurna I, Machhapuchhre, Lamjung, and Manaslu).

name: Kushma Bungee & Suspension Bridges
short_description: Home to one of the world's highest bungee jumps and longest suspension bridges.
full_description: Kushma is famous for its 228m bungee jump over the Kali Gandaki River (The Cliff), as well as the Kushma-Gyadi suspension bridge (one of Nepal's highest). It is a premier adventure destination.

name: Baglung Kalika Temple
short_description: A highly revered Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Kali.
full_description: Located in the middle of a dense forest on the banks of the Kaligandaki River, this temple is a major pilgrimage site, especially during Dashain and Chaite Dashain festivals.

name: Devghat Dham
short_description: A sacred confluence of Trisuli and Kaligandaki rivers.
full_description: One of the most sacred places in Hindu mythology, Devghat is a center for Sanskrit education and features numerous ashrams, temples, and caves where sages are said to have meditated.

name: Amaltari Homestay
short_description: A Tharu community homestay located in the buffer zone of Chitwan National Park.
full_description: Amaltari offers a mix of wildlife adventure and indigenous Tharu culture. Visitors can stay in traditional mud-and-thatch houses and participate in community forest safaris.

name: Chame
short_description: The administrative headquarters of Manang district along the Marsyangdi River.
full_description: Chame serves as a major pitstop on the Annapurna Circuit. It is famous for its natural hot springs and offers stunning views of Lamjung Himal and Annapurna II.

name: Upper Pisang
short_description: A scenic village offering some of the best views of the Annapurna range.
full_description: Upper Pisang features a beautiful monastery and offers a superior panoramic view of Annapurna II compared to the lower village. It is a key settlement on the high-route trek to Manang.

name: Ghorepani
short_description: A popular trekking crossroad famous for its rhododendron forests.
full_description: Ghorepani was historically a rest stop for horse traders. Today, it is a bustling trekker hub and the gateway to Poon Hill, surrounded by the world's largest rhododendron forest.

name: Poon Hill
short_description: One of the most iconic Himalayan sunrise viewpoints in Nepal.
full_description: Located at 3,210 meters, Poon Hill offers a 360-degree panorama of Dhaulagiri, Annapurna South, Machhapuchhre, and Nilgiri. It is the primary destination of the Ghorepani-Poon Hill trek.

name: Tatopani (Myagdi)
short_description: Famous for its natural riverside hot springs beside the Kali Gandaki.
full_description: A key stop on the Annapurna Circuit and Jomsom trek. The name 'Tatopani' literally means 'Hot Water', referring to the natural springs which are believed to have healing properties.

name: Landruk
short_description: A beautiful Magar village facing Ghandruk across the Modi Khola.
full_description: Landruk is a picturesque settlement known for its terraced fields and Magar heritage. It is a major stop for trekkers coming from Dhampus or heading towards Annapurna Base Camp.

name: Sarangkot
short_description: The most popular hilltop viewpoint near Pokhara city.
full_description: Sarangkot is world-renowned for its paragliding takeoff sites. It provides a stunning view of Pokhara Valley and the Annapurna range, especially at sunrise and sunset.

name: Besisahar
short_description: The gateway town for the world-famous Annapurna Circuit trek.
full_description: Besisahar is the district headquarters of Lamjung. It is a vibrant trading hub where most trekkers begin their journey along the Marsyangdi River valley.

name: Samagaun
short_description: A high-altitude Tibetan-style village at the foot of Mount Manaslu.
full_description: Samagaun is a key settlement on the Manaslu Circuit. It features the Pungyen Gompa and is the primary staging ground for expeditions to the summit of Manaslu.

name: Tsum Valley
short_description: A sacred Himalayan pilgrimage valley known as the 'Hidden Valley'.
full_description: Tsum Valley is a culturally distinct region where the killing of animals is prohibited by tradition. It is home to ancient monasteries like Mu Gompa and Rachen Gompa.

name: Larkya La Pass
short_description: The highest point of the Manaslu Circuit Trek at 5,106 meters.
full_description: Larkya La is a challenging mountain pass that connects the Gorkha and Manang districts. It offers dramatic views of Larkya Peak, Himlung Himal, and Cheo Himal.

name: Siddha Gufa
short_description: One of the largest caves in Nepal.
full_description: Located near Bandipur, Siddha Gufa is 437 meters deep and 50 meters high. It features stunning stalactites and stalagmites and offers a thrilling underground experience.

name: Khopra Danda
short_description: An off-the-beaten-path ridge offering close-up views of Dhaulagiri.
full_description: A community-managed trekking route that offers eye-level views of Mount Dhaulagiri. It is an alternative to the crowded Poon Hill and serves as a base for hikes to Khayer Lake.

name: Khayer Lake
short_description: A sacred alpine lake at 4,600 meters, believed to fulfill wishes.
full_description: A day-hike from Khopra Danda, this pristine lake is a pilgrimage site for locals. It offers complete isolation and incredible views of the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri massifs.

name: Kapuche Glacier Lake
short_description: One of the lowest altitude glacier lakes in the world (2,546m).
full_description: Kapuche is a stunning turquoise lake formed by glacial melt from Annapurna II and Lamjung Himal. The trek from Sikles takes only 1-2 days, making it one of the most accessible glacial lakes in Nepal.

name: Nar Phu Valley
short_description: A restricted 'Lost Valley' featuring medieval Tibetan culture.
full_description: Nar and Phu are two remote villages in a hidden valley north of the Annapurna Circuit. Requiring a special permit, the area is famous for its ancient stone houses, high-altitude monasteries, and a culture that has remained unchanged for centuries.

name: World Peace Pagoda
short_description: A brilliant white stupa on Anadu Hill overlooking Phewa Lake and the Annapurna range.
full_description: Built by Buddhist monks from the Japanese Nipponzan Myohoji organization, this massive Peace Pagoda offers arguably the most iconic view of Pokhara Valley, Phewa Lake, and Mount Machhapuchhre.

name: Davis Falls (Patale Chhango)
short_description: A unique waterfall that flows directly into a narrow, deep subterranean canal.
full_description: Known locally as Patale Chhango (Underworld Waterfall), the water from Phewa Lake forms a dramatic cascade before plunging into a deep gorge and disappearing underground.

name: Gupteshwor Mahadev Cave
short_description: A deep, spiraling cave system housing a sacred Shiva lingam.
full_description: Located directly across the street from Davis Falls, this is Nepal's most famous religious cave. The lower depths provide a dramatic internal view of the roaring waters of Davis Falls.

name: Gorkha Durbar
short_description: The historical fort, palace, and temple complex of King Prithvi Narayan Shah.
full_description: Perched on a ridge, this 16th-century architectural marvel is the birthplace of unified Nepal. It combines a fort, a palace, and the Kalika Temple.

name: Manakamana Temple
short_description: The sacred temple of the wish-fulfilling Hindu goddess, accessible by Nepal's first cable car.
full_description: Situated on a prominent ridge, Manakamana is one of the most important pilgrimage sites in Nepal. Devotees believe the goddess Bhagwati grants wishes.

name: Phewa Lake
short_description: The second largest lake in Nepal and the primary tourism hub of Pokhara.
full_description: Phewa Lake is famous for the mirror-like reflection of Mount Machhapuchhre (Fishtail) and the Annapurna range on its surface. The north shore, known as 'Lakeside', is a vibrant tourist district.

name: Tal Barahi Temple
short_description: A two-story pagoda temple situated on a small island in the center of Phewa Lake.
full_description: Dedicated to Goddess Barahi (an incarnation of Durga), this is the most important religious monument in Pokhara. It can only be reached by boat.

name: Mahendra Cave
short_description: A large limestone cave complex in the northern part of Pokhara.
full_description: Discovered in the 1950s, Mahendra Cave is known for its stalactite and stalagmite rock formations. The cave is well-lit and contains a statue of Lord Shiva.

name: Seti River Gorge
short_description: A mysterious gorge where the Seti River disappears deep into the earth.
full_description: Flowing straight through Pokhara, the milky-white Seti River cuts a deep and narrow gorge. In some places, the river is barely visible, running nearly 50 meters below the city's surface.

name: Bindhyabasini Temple
short_description: The oldest temple in Pokhara, dedicated to Goddess Bhagwati.
full_description: Perched on a small hill in the old bazaar area, this white pagoda-style temple is a focal point of religious life in Pokhara. The temple courtyard offers a panoramic view of the Annapurna range.

name: International Mountain Museum
short_description: A comprehensive museum documenting the history of the Himalayas and global mountaineering.
full_description: Operated by the Nepal Mountaineering Association, this museum chronicles the world's highest peaks, the flora and fauna of the Himalayas, the culture of mountain people, and the history of historic expeditions.

name: Panchakot
short_description: A grand religious site featuring a 108-foot tall Kalash (pot).
full_description: Panchakot is an ambitious religious tourism project featuring massive statues, a 108-foot tall Kalash, and several temples built in different architectural styles. It offers a 360-degree view of the Dhaulagiri and Annapurna ranges.

name: Gandaki Golden Bridge
short_description: The longest suspension bridge in Nepal (567m).
full_description: Often called the 'Single Span Suspension Bridge,' it connects Baglung's Aduwabari with Parbat's Pang. Spanning 567 meters in length and hanging 122 meters above the Kali Gandaki River.

name: Galeshwor Dham
short_description: A sacred temple complex built upon a single massive rock on the banks of the Kali Gandaki.
full_description: Often called the 'Gateway to Muktinath', Galeshwor is where the throat of Sati Devi is said to have fallen. The temple is unique because it is built entirely on a sprawling 9-ropani single rock.

name: Kokhe Danda
short_description: A high viewpoint (3,303m) offering 360-degree views of Dhaulagiri and Annapurna.
full_description: A rising star for off-the-beaten-path trekkers, Kokhe Danda provides an unobstructed panoramic view of Dhaulagiri, Annapurna, and Machhapuchhre. It is far less crowded than Poon Hill.

name: Khumai Hill
short_description: A popular short trek from Pokhara offering close views of Machhapuchhre.
full_description: Khumai Hill (3,245m) has quickly become a favorite for locals and domestic tourists. It offers an incredibly close view of the Fishtail mountain. The trek is typically done in 2-3 days from Pokhara.

name: Karpu Danda
short_description: A peaceful viewpoint near Tangting village with spectacular sunrise views.
full_description: Often called the 'Hidden Jewel of Kaski', Karpu Danda (3,000m) is accessible via a trek from the Gurung village of Tangting. It provides an unmatched view of Lamjung Himal and Annapurna II.

name: Tangting Village
short_description: A scenic Gurung settlement located directly across the valley from Sikles.
full_description: Often visited in tandem with Sikles, Tangting is a quieter, incredibly charming village. It provides an uninterrupted, eye-level panorama of Lamjung Himal and Annapurna IV.

name: Muri Village
short_description: A major stop on the Dhaulagiri Circuit Trek.
full_description: Muri is a Magar settlement that offers spectacular views of Mount Dhaulagiri. It is one of the last permanent settlements before entering the high glaciated zones of the Dhaulagiri massif.

name: Narchyang Waterfall
short_description: A massive waterfall located in the Narchyang village.
full_description: Falling from the glaciers of Annapurna, this waterfall is becoming a hotspot for canyoning. The village of Narchyang also offers one of the best community homestay experiences in Myagdi.

name: Dana Gorge
short_description: Recognized as the deepest gorge in the world.
full_description: The Kali Gandaki river carves a massive gorge between the towering 8,000-meter peaks of Dhaulagiri and Annapurna I. The viewpoint near Dana village offers a dizzying look into this geographical marvel.

name: Rupse Waterfall
short_description: A stunning 300-meter waterfall along the Beni-Jomsom road.
full_description: One of the most beautiful waterfalls in Nepal, Rupse Jharna is a popular stop for travelers heading to Mustang. It is located near the world's deepest gorge, Dana.

name: Todke Hill
short_description: A 360-degree panoramic viewpoint at 2,500 meters.
full_description: Todke offers views of Dhaulagiri, Annapurna, and Manaslu. It is famous for its dense rhododendron forests and quiet trekking trails.

name: Muldhai Viewpoint (Dobato)
short_description: A less-crowded alternative to Poon Hill at 3,637 meters.
full_description: Located above Dobato on the Khopra Ridge trek, Muldhai offers a more expansive 360-degree panorama of the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri ranges than Poon Hill.

name: Chhusang Village
short_description: A dramatic village surrounded by towering red cliffs and ancient cave dwellings.
full_description: Chhusang lies at the junction of the Narshing Khola and the Kali Gandaki River. It is famous for its organic apple orchards, spectacular eroded red cliffs, and sky caves high above the village.

name: Tsarang (Charang) Village
short_description: The ancient capital of Mustang featuring a historic palace and massive red Gompa.
full_description: Tsarang is a labyrinth of stone walls and alleys. The village is dominated by a 5-story white dzong (palace) and a massive 14th-century red monastery housing the largest library in Lo.

name: Ghami Village
short_description: The third largest village in Upper Mustang, famous for Nepal's longest Mani wall.
full_description: Ghami is a picturesque village surrounded by extensive fields. Just outside the village lies a spectacular 305-meter-long Mani wall (prayer wall), painted in red, white, and black.

name: Luri Gompa
short_description: A 13th-century cave monastery with rare Buddhist murals.
full_description: A site in Upper Mustang, Luri Gompa is built inside a sandstone cliff. It contains unique mandalas and statues that are among the best preserved in the Tibetan Buddhist world.

name: Dhumba Lake
short_description: A sacred turquoise lake near Jomsom.
full_description: Located at the base of Mount Nilgiri, the lake is sacred to Buddhists. It is known for its clear water that reflects the mountain.

name: Thini Village
short_description: One of the oldest Thakali villages in Mustang.
full_description: Thini is located just above Jomsom. It offers an authentic Thakali cultural experience, ancient Bön monasteries, and a stunning view of the Kali Gandaki valley.

name: Lubra Village
short_description: A hidden village that is one of the few centers of the Bön religion.
full_description: Located in a side valley between Jomsom and Muktinath, Lubra is a rare Bön settlement. It remains largely untouched by modern tourism.

name: Dharapani
short_description: A major junction where the Manaslu Circuit and Annapurna Circuit meet.
full_description: Dharapani is a bustling trekking hub located along the Marsyangdi River. It marks the point where trekkers finishing the Manaslu Circuit join the main trail of the Annapurna Circuit.

name: Ngawal Village
short_description: A beautifully preserved Tibetan-style village on the upper route of the Annapurna Circuit.
full_description: Located at 3,660 meters, Ngawal features flat-roofed stone houses and ancient Gompas. Taking the upper trail to Ngawal offers far superior views of the Annapurna range.

name: Ice Lake (Kicho Tal)
short_description: A stunning high-altitude lake used as a major acclimatization hike from Manang.
full_description: Situated at 4,600 meters, Ice Lake is a challenging day hike from Manang or Braga. The lake remains frozen for much of the year and offers incredible panoramas of Annapurna II, III, IV, and Gangapurna.

name: Philim Village
short_description: A large, prosperous Gurung village on the Manaslu Circuit.
full_description: Philim is a major village in the lower Manaslu region known for its neatly terraced fields of millet and maize. It features a Japanese-built school and serves as the gateway to the Tsum Valley.

name: Namrung Village
short_description: The entry point to the upper Nupri region with strong Tibetan influences.
full_description: At 2,660 meters, Namrung is where the Manaslu trek transitions into high-altitude Tibetan culture. It offers the first good views of Siring Himal, Ganesh Himal, and Mount Manaslu.

name: Lho Village
short_description: A stunning alpine village dominated by the Ribung Gompa.
full_description: Lho (3,180m) is famous for its massive Ribung Monastery and the incredible sunrise view over Mount Manaslu. The village is adorned with prayer flags, mani walls, and chortens.

name: Jagat (Gorkha)
short_description: A stone-paved village and the official entry to the Manaslu Conservation Area.
full_description: Jagat is a well-maintained village with a unique flagstone-paved main street. It serves as the primary check-post for Manaslu restricted area permits and MCAP entry.

name: Dhorbarahi Temple
short_description: A mysterious temple with a natural 'ebb and flow' water pond.
full_description: Dedicated to Goddess Barahi, this temple is famous for its pond where water levels mysteriously rise and fall at intervals. It is nestled in a peaceful forested gorge.

name: Kaskikot
short_description: The ancestral seat of the Kaski Kingdom.
full_description: Kaskikot holds the ruins of the ancient palace of the Shah kings. It offers a unique vantage point where you can see the Annapurna range on one side and Phewa Lake on the other.

name: Kahun Danda
short_description: A historical hilltop offering panoramic views of Pokhara valley and the Annapurnas.
full_description: Less crowded than Sarangkot, Kahun Danda features a view tower, the ruins of a 200-year-old fort (Kot), and excellent trails for short day hikes directly from the center of Pokhara.

name: Pame
short_description: A tranquil lakeside area famous for its local fish delicacies.
full_description: Located at the far end of Phewa Lake, Pame is a quiet retreat away from the main Lakeside. It is renowned for its traditional restaurants serving fresh fish caught from the lake.

name: Pumdikot Shiva Statue
short_description: Home to a 51-foot tall statue of Lord Shiva above the Peace Pagoda.
full_description: Pumdikot is an emerging religious tourism hub featuring a 51-foot tall statue of Lord Shiva sitting on a massive dome. It offers sweeping 360-degree views of the Annapurna massif and the Pokhara Valley.

name: Matepani Gumba
short_description: A beautifully ornate Buddhist monastery on a hilltop in east Pokhara.
full_description: Established by the Nyingma sect, Matepani Gumba features grand statues of Lord Buddha, Guru Rinpoche, and Karmapa. The interior is decorated with intricate murals.

name: Bat Cave (Chamero Gufa)
short_description: A dark, natural cave famous for housing thousands of Horseshoe bats.
full_description: Located near Mahendra Cave, the Bat Cave is largely unlit and rugged, offering a more adventurous caving experience. The exit requires climbing through a notoriously tight rock fissure.

name: Bimalnagar Rock Climbing
short_description: One of Nepal's premier natural rock climbing destinations.
full_description: Located along the Prithvi Highway near Bandipur, Bimalnagar features a massive limestone cliff face with over 20 bolted routes of varying difficulties.

name: Maharajathan Temple
short_description: An ancient Shakti Peeth established during the Sen Dynasty.
full_description: Located in Hattikhor, this temple is believed to have been founded by King Manimukunda Sen of Palpa. It is a major center for the indigenous Tharu community.

name: Triveni Dham (Nawalpur)
short_description: A major pilgrimage site at the confluence of Narayani, Sona, and Tamasa rivers.
full_description: Triveni is mentioned in the Ramayana as the location of Valmiki Rishi's ashram. It is a key site for Maghe Sankranti festivals.

name: Shashwat Dham
short_description: A world-class spiritual destination and architectural marvel.
full_description: Spread over 12 acres, Shashwat Dham features the stunning Ekambareshwar Shiva Temple built in Khajuraho style. It includes a Vedic school, a digital museum, and a syncretic garden.

name: Devchuli Hill
short_description: The highest peak in Nawalpur district (1,937m) offering views of Terai plains and Himalayas.
full_description: A trek to the summit reveals dense forests rich in flora and fauna, and an unparalleled view of the Narayani River valley to the south and the Annapurna range to the north.

name: Daunne Devi Temple
short_description: A historic temple located on a hill along the Mahendra Highway.
full_description: Daunne Devi is a famous stop for travelers on the East-West highway. The temple complex offers a peaceful environment and views of the surrounding hills and plains.

name: Ligligkot
short_description: The site of the historic race to become the King of Liglig.
full_description: In ancient times, the king of Liglig was chosen by a race from Chepeghat to the top of the hill. Today, it remains a historical site with a fortress ruin and holds an annual marathon.

name: Kaulepani
short_description: Award-winning homestay village known for its hospitality.
full_description: Located near Besisahar, Kaulepani is a mixed community of Gurung, Magar, and Newar people. It has been recognized as a top village tourism destination in Nepal.

name: Khasur Village
short_description: A traditional Gurung village offering an authentic homestay experience near Besisahar.
full_description: Khasur is an easily accessible village that has maintained its rich Gurung heritage. Visitors are welcomed with traditional music and organic local food.

name: Waling Valley
short_description: A long, fertile valley known as the 'smart city' of Nepal.
full_description: Waling is a beautiful longitudinal valley along the Aandhikhola river. It's famous for its neatness, orange production, and the legend of Shravan Kumar from the Ramayana.

name: Alamdevi Temple
short_description: The ancestral deity temple of the Shah kings of Nepal.
full_description: Alamdevi is a highly sacred site for the Shah dynasty. It is a 'gupta' (hidden) temple where only the designated priests and family members of the royal bloodline were historically allowed to enter.

name: Swarek Maidan
short_description: A premier paragliding take-off spot in Syangja.
full_description: Swarek Maidan is a massive hilltop flatland. It has hosted international paragliding competitions and is considered one of the best spots for thermal flying outside of Pokhara.

name: Setibeni
short_description: Home to the world's largest Shaligram stone.
full_description: Located at the confluence of the Kali Gandaki and Seti rivers, Setibeni houses a massive black stone revered as a manifestation of Lord Vishnu. It is a major pilgrimage site.

name: Bhirkot Palace
short_description: A historic palace representing one of the prominent Chaubise principalities.
full_description: Bhirkot holds great significance in medieval Nepalese history as a powerful Sen kingdom before the unification. It remains a vital historical landmark for the Syangja district.

name: Chhangchhangdi (Chhayakshetra)
short_description: A prominent religious site associated with the Swasthani Brata Katha.
full_description: According to Hindu mythology, this is the spot where the last organ of Goddess Sati Devi fell while Lord Shiva was carrying her body. It features a temple complex and a sacred water sprout.

name: Nuwakot (Syangja)
short_description: A historic hilltop fort and a popular paragliding spot.
full_description: Not to be confused with Nuwakot near Kathmandu, this site in Syangja features an ancient fort and offers views of the Annapurna range. It is a burgeoning site for adventure sports.

name: Kaligandaki A Hydro Dam
short_description: One of Nepal's largest hydroelectric projects.
full_description: This engineering feat on the Kali Gandaki River created a large reservoir that has become a spot for local sightseeing. It showcases Nepal's progress in renewable energy.

name: Maharani Than
short_description: A rising religious destination in Myagdi, associated with a historical queen.
full_description: Located in Beni, this temple complex has gained national attention following the publication of the Madan Puraskar-winning novel 'Maharani'. It is dedicated to a historical queen-turned-deity.

name: Lovely Hill (Beni)
short_description: A popular local viewpoint overlooking Beni Bazaar and the river confluence.
full_description: A short walk from Beni Bazaar, Lovely Hill has been developed with stone-paved paths and resting areas. It provides a beautiful view of the confluence of the Kali Gandaki and Myagdi rivers.

name: Alapeshwar Cave
short_description: A rugged, less-explored cave with beautiful stalactites.
full_description: Located near the banks of the Kali Gandaki, Alapeshwar is more natural and less commercialized than Gupteshwor. It features impressive limestone formations.

name: Bhalu Pahad
short_description: A famous winding cliff on the Siddhartha Highway.
full_description: Known as 'Bear Hill,' this is a popular stop for travelers between Pokhara and Syangja. The cliff offers a dramatic look at the winding highway below and the lush green hills.

name: Dhyanakyu Village
short_description: A tranquil, hidden village on the Annapurna Circuit.
full_description: Often overlooked by trekkers rushing to Chame, Dhyanakyu is a peaceful hamlet surrounded by pine forests. It offers a natural amphitheater view of Lamjung Himal and Annapurna II.

name: Bahunthan Hill
short_description: A viewpoint overlooking Putalibazar and the Aandhikhola valley.
full_description: Bahunthan features a temple and a viewpoint that provides a perfect aerial view of the Syangja district headquarters and the surrounding terraced hills.

name: Gahrou Kalika
short_description: An ancient hilltop temple dedicated to Goddess Kalika.
full_description: Located above the town of Waling, this temple is a significant spiritual center for the people of Syangja. It offers great views of the Aandhikhola valley and the distant Himalayas.

name: Suntalabari
short_description: The 'Orange Orchard' village of Syangja.
full_description: True to its name, Suntalabari is famous for its massive orange production. During winter (Nov-Jan), the entire village turns orange with ripe fruit, making it a popular spot for agrotourism.

name: Chapakot Valley
short_description: A large, flat river valley in south Syangja.
full_description: Chapakot is one of the most fertile plains in the district. Surrounded by the Kali Gandaki river, it is a hub for agriculture and offers a serene, rural landscape.

name: Daru Shiruwari
short_description: An emerging community-based homestay village.
full_description: Similar to Sirubari, Daru Shiruwari offers an authentic glimpse into the Magar and Gurung lifestyles. It is part of Syangja's push for sustainable rural tourism.

name: Bhimad
short_description: An ancient river-confluence market town.
full_description: Located at the confluence of the Seti and Jhimruk rivers, Bhimad is a historic trading center for the southern part of Tanahu. It serves as a gateway to the southern hills of Tanahu and Syangja.
